EUV / Lithography.desc: EUV光刻 — Sole-source monopoly, most capital-intensive layer

Etch & Deposition.desc: Etch, CVD, PVD — critical patterning steps

EDA & Design Software.desc: EDA tools, IC design verification, simulation

Packaging & Assembly.desc: OSAT, probe cards, advanced packaging test

ASML.name: ASML Holding N.V.
ASML.thesis: Sole EUV lithography supplier; monopoly pricing power; every leading-edge fab must buy ASML. High-NA EUV ramp is next growth driver.

ONTO.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
ONTO.thesis: Lithography overlay metrology; benefits from EUV multi-patterning complexity. Small but fast-growing.

LRCX.name: Lam Research Corporation
LRCX.thesis: Etch & deposition leader; critical for 3D NAND and advanced logic. Strong recurring revenue from installed base.

AMAT.name: Applied Materials, Inc.
AMAT.thesis: Largest WFE company by revenue; diversified across etch, CVD, PVD, CMP. Benefits from every node transition.

KLAC.name: KLA Corporation
KLAC.thesis: ~50% market share in process control; yield is non-negotiable at advanced nodes. High-margin, asset-light model.

TER.name: Teradyne, Inc.
TER.thesis: ATE market leader; positioned for HPC/AI chip testing complexity growth. Robotics segment adds diversification.

COHR.name: Coherent, Inc.
COHR.thesis: Lasers and optoelectronics; critical for datacom transceivers and advanced packaging. AI datacenter tailwind.

IPGP.name: IPG Photonics Corporation
IPGP.thesis: Fiber laser pioneer; industrial applications + emerging semi use cases. Vertically integrated advantage.

ENTG.name: Entegris, Inc.
ENTG.thesis: Critical materials purity leader; filters/chemicals for advanced nodes. Sticky customer relationships.

MKSI.name: MKS Inc.
MKSI.thesis: Subsystems/vacuum/laser; broad semi exposure. Acquired Atotech for advanced packaging chemistry.

CAMT.name: Camtek Ltd.
CAMT.thesis: Camtek — inspection/metrology for advanced packaging. HBM and heterogeneous integration tailwind.

UCTT.name: Ultra Clean Holdings, Inc.
UCTT.thesis: Ultra Clean Holdings — gas delivery, chambers, parts. Leveraged to WFE cycle upturn.

SNPS.name: Synopsys, Inc.
SNPS.thesis: EDA duopoly with Cadence; essential for chip design. AI-driven design tools accelerate adoption.

CDNS.name: Cadence Design Systems, Inc.
CDNS.thesis: #2 EDA; strong in verification/emulation. Expanding into system design and computational fluid dynamics.

ANSS.name: ANSYS, Inc.
ANSS.thesis: Simulation software; increasingly critical for chip-package-system co-design. Synopsys acquisition target.

AMKR.name: Amkor Technology, Inc.
AMKR.thesis: Largest pure-play OSAT; advanced packaging (2.5D/3D) for AI chips. Key TSMC CoWoS partner.

AEHR.name: Aehr Test Systems
AEHR.thesis: Niche test systems for SiC and GaN power devices. Automotive/EV electrification tailwind.

FORM.name: FormFactor, Inc.
FORM.thesis: Probe card leader; essential for wafer-level testing. Benefits from rising test complexity.

TSM.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited
TSM.thesis: World's leading foundry; N2 GAA ramp. Every major AI chip fabbed at TSMC. Irreplaceable.

INTC.name: Intel Corporation
INTC.thesis: Intel foundry services + IDM. 18A process could close gap. Turnaround potential but execution risk.

GFS.name: GLOBALFOUNDRIES Inc.
GFS.thesis: Specialty/mature node foundry; automotive, IoT, RF. Less capex-intensive, steady cash flows.

UMC.name: United Microelectronics Corporation
UMC.thesis: Mature node foundry; 28nm sweet spot for IoT/auto. High dividend yield for a semi company.

BRKS.name: Brooks Automation, Inc.
BRKS.thesis: Azenta — automation/cryo-biology; semi automation + life sciences. Diversified revenue.

ICHR.name: Ichor Holdings, Ltd.
ICHR.thesis: Ichor Holdings — gas/chemical delivery subsystems. Pure-play on WFE spending recovery.

bottlenecks.name: EUV Lithography Capacity
bottlenecks.desc: ASML sole supplier; High-NA EUV lead times >18 months. Single point of failure for N2/A14 ramps.

bottlenecks.name: Advanced Packaging (CoWoS)
bottlenecks.desc: TSMC CoWoS capacity constraints for AI chips. 2-year lead time for HBM integration.

bottlenecks.name: HBM Supply
bottlenecks.desc: SK Hynix/Samsung HBM3E allocation tight. Equipment for HBM stacking is bottleneck.

bottlenecks.name: Etch Tool Delivery
bottlenecks.desc: Lam/AMAT etch tool lead times 6-9 months. Critical for 3D NAND 300+ layers.

bottlenecks.name: EDA Compute Resources
bottlenecks.desc: AI chip design requires massive EDA compute. Cloud EDA adoption accelerating.
